texto: (from a prayer for inclusiveness):O god whose face changes as we move and learn and change, whose image becomes less like ours and more like that of the stranger we treat as a friend; the god whom we create from the sum of all we know that is wonderful, generous, true and wise: May we see ourselves in the god-ness of others and ourselves in their image of you.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Bronwyn Angela White: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amizade. O trecho “(from a prayer for inclusiveness):O god whose face changes as we move and learn and change, whose…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, create, god-ness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e termino.
